description: List zone data. If no keyword is given, the default output of the command is that associated with the information keyword.

6.0.description: List zone data. If no keyword is given, the default output of the command is that associated with the information keyword.

keywords.name: density
keywords.description: Zone mass density.

keywords.name: extra
keywords.description: Extra zone variable for extra array index i.

keywords.name: gridpoint
keywords.description: ID number of gridpoint associated with zones.

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: Zone group assignments. Only groups in slot s will be shown if the slot keyword is used.

keywords.name: information
keywords.description: general zone information: the zone type (brick, wedge, etc), the constitutive model assigned, density, volume, and centroid.

keywords.name: join
keywords.description: Zone face (join) connections

keywords.name: mechanical
keywords.description: General information about zone mechanical calculations. Large strain setting, nodal mixed discretization setting, damping settings, location of maximum local force ratio, and others.

keywords.name: pore-pressure
keywords.description: Zone pore pressure (average from gridpoint values)

keywords.name: principal
keywords.description: Principal stress components for zones. The default output is total.

keywords.name: principal-effective
keywords.description: Effective principal stress.

keywords.name: principal-total
keywords.description: Total principal stress.

keywords.name: profile
keywords.description: List the values of a zone field variable on points spaced equally over a line.

keywords.name: profile-condition
keywords.description: a measure of how badly deformed a zone is (see Condition Measure of Zones).

keywords.name: profile-density
keywords.description: The density of the zone.

keywords.name: profile-displacement
keywords.description: displacement magnitude at the gridpoint

keywords.name: profile-displacement-x
keywords.description: x-displacement at the gridpoint

keywords.name: profile-displacement-y
keywords.description: y-displacement at the gridpoint

keywords.name: profile-displacement-z
keywords.description: z-displacement at the gridpoint

keywords.name: profile-extra
keywords.description: extra variable value. The extra variable index used will default to 1 and can be changed with the index modifier keyword. The value will by default come from the grid point extra variables, but this can be specified using the source modifier keyword. The value will by default be treated as a scalar floating point type, but this can be specified using the type modifier keyword.

keywords.name: profile-pore-pressure
keywords.description: pore pressure in zone. By default this will be the grid point pore pressure, but the zone average pressure can be specified by using the source keyword modifier.

keywords.name: profile-property
keywords.description: a property of the mechanical constitutive model of the zone. The property name must be specified using the name keyword modifier. By default it will be assumed the property is a floating point scalar, but this can be specified using the type modifier keyword.

keywords.name: profile-property-fluid
keywords.description: a property of the fluid constitutive model of the zone. The property name must be specified using the name keyword modifier. By default it will be assumed the property is a floating point scalar, but this can be specified using the type modifier keyword.

keywords.name: profile-property-thermal
keywords.description: a property of the thermal constitutive model of the zone. The property name must be specified using the name keyword modifier. By default it will be assumed the property is a floating point scalar, but this can be specified using the type modifier keyword.

keywords.name: profile-ratio-local
keywords.description: the local unbalanced force ratio at each gridpoint. Like all results, this can be changed to the logarithm of the value by using the:lkwd:’log’ keyword modifier.

keywords.name: profile-strain-increment
keywords.description: the strain increment tensor of the zone, determined by the current displacement field. Use the quantity keyword to specify which scalar value to retrieve from the tensor.

keywords.name: profile-strain-rate
keywords.description: the strain rate tensor of the zone, determined by the current velocity field. Use the quantity keyword to specify which scalar value to retrieve from the tensor.

keywords.name: profile-stress
keywords.description: the stress tensor of the zone, determined by the weighted average of the subzone stresses. Use the quantity keyword to specify which scalar value to retrieve from the tensor.

keywords.name: profile-stress-effective
keywords.description: the effective stress tensor of the zone, determined by the weighted average of the subzone stresses minus the zone averaged pore pressure. Use the quantity keyword to specify which scalar value to retrieve from the tensor.

keywords.name: profile-stress-strength-ratio
keywords.description: the ratio of the current stress to a failure surface of the zone, as determined by the current zone constitutive model. Not all constitutive models support this calculation. If unsupported, the value returned will be 10. The value returned is generally held to a maximum of 10.

keywords.name: profile-temperature
keywords.description: the temperature at the gridpoints. The source modifier keyword can be used to specify that zone based temperature should be used instead.

keywords.name: profile-unbalanced-force
keywords.description: the unbalanced force magnitude at the gridpoint.

keywords.name: profile-unbalanced-force-x
keywords.description: x-unbalanced force at the gridpoint.

keywords.name: profile-unbalanced-force-y
keywords.description: y-unbalanced force at the gridpoint

keywords.name: profile-unbalanced-force-z
keywords.description: z-unbalanced force at the gridpoint

keywords.name: profile-velocity
keywords.description: the velocity magnitude at the gridpoint

keywords.name: profile-velocity-x
keywords.description: x-velocity at the gridpoint

keywords.name: profile-velocity-y
keywords.description: y-velocity at the gridpoint

keywords.name: profile-velocity-z
keywords.description: z-velocity at the gridpoint

keywords.name: property
keywords.description: Material properties assigned to zones. Values are displayed for the property named s. Property names for each constitutive model are listed with the model definitions (see the model defintions in the Constitutive Models section).

keywords.name: state
keywords.description: Plasticity state indicators. The default keyword is any.

keywords.name: state-any
keywords.description: Lists the union of all plasticity state flags in any tetrahedral subzone (default).

keywords.name: state-average
keywords.description: Prints plasticity state if more than 50% of tetrahedral subzones are at this state.

keywords.name: strain
keywords.description: lists shear strain rate, shear strain incrmement, volumetric strain rate and volumetric strain increment. Strain rates are basec on the current velocity field, and strain increments are based on the current displacement field.

keywords.name: strain-increment
keywords.description: the strain increment tensor, based on the current displacement field.

keywords.name: strain-rate
keywords.description: the strain rate tensor, based on the current velocity field.

keywords.name: stress
keywords.description: components of total stress for zones.

keywords.name: stress-effective
keywords.description: components of effective stress for zones.

keywords.name: summary
keywords.description: summary of zone module status.

keywords.name: tetrahedra
keywords.description: List values at each tetrahedral subzone of the zone.

keywords.name: tetrahedra-gridpoint
keywords.description: The four gridpoints associated with each subzone.

keywords.name: tetrahedra-information
keywords.description: The volume and centroid location of each subzone.

keywords.name: tetrahedra-principal
keywords.description: The current principal stresses of each subzone, including directions.

keywords.name: tetrahedra-principal-effective
keywords.description: The current principal effective stresses of each subzone, including directions.

keywords.name: tetrahedra-stress
keywords.description: The current stress tensor in each subzone.

keywords.name: tetrahedra-stress-effective
keywords.description: The current effective stress tensor in each subzone.

keywords.name: begin
keywords.description: Specifies the beginning point of the profile line.

keywords.name: component
keywords.description: This keyword selects which scalar to retrieve from a vector type value, such as velocity or displacement. If the value type is not vector, this setting is ignored. The available options are:

keywords.name: component-x
keywords.description: x-component of the vector

keywords.name: component-y
keywords.description: y-component of the vector

keywords.name: component-z
keywords.description: z-component of the vector

keywords.name: component-magnitude
keywords.description: vector magnitude

keywords.name: end
keywords.description: Specifies the ending point of the profile line.

keywords.name: index
keywords.description: For keywords that require it (most notably extra), this specifies the index that should be used. The default value will be 1.

keywords.name: gravity
keywords.description: If on, then gravitational forces are included in the returned applied force. The default is off.

keywords.name: gridpointid
keywords.description: Specifies the location of the history by the location of the gridpoint with ID number i.

keywords.name: log
keywords.description: If on, the returned number is the base 10 log of the absolute value of the original value. The default is off.

keywords.name: method
keywords.description: This specifies the method of calculation used to bring zone-based quantities to arbitrary locations in space (including at gridpoint positions).

keywords.name: method-constant
keywords.description: Assumes values are constant inside zones. This is the default option. Values at gridpoints or at zone boundaries will pick one adjacent zone to use.

keywords.name: method-average
keywords.description: This uses an volume weighted average of all the zones connected to the gridpoint.

keywords.name: method-inverse-distance-weight
keywords.description: This uses an weighted average of up to 32 zones in the immediate vicinity of the point. The weighting is determined by a power of the distance of the zone centroid from the specified point.

keywords.name: method-inverse-distance-weight-power
keywords.description: Specifies the power of the distance used to weight values. The default power is 3.0.

keywords.name: method-inverse-distance-weight-radius-ratio
keywords.description: Specifies the ratio of the maximum distance from the point of the zones selected, to the ratio used to calculate weighted values. Zones farther from this value are discounted. The default value is 0.75.

keywords.name: method-polynomial
keywords.description: Uses a fit to a polynomial function based on the zones in the vicinity of the point. Degenerate degrees of freedom are detected and removed. The optional tolerance flag can be used to change the value used to detect a degenerancy. The default value is 1e-7.

keywords.name: name
keywords.description: Certain kewords (most notably property) require a name value to be fully specified. This will determine the specific property name retrieved.

keywords.name: null
keywords.description: Specifies the criteria used to ignore zone values. By default mechanical null zones are ignored.

keywords.name: null-fluid
keywords.description: Zones with a null fluid constitutive model are also ignored if this is set to on.

keywords.name: null-mechanical
keywords.description: Zones with a null mechanical constitutive model are ignored if this is set to on (this is the default).

keywords.name: null-thermal
keywords.description: Zones with a null thermal constitutive model are ignored if this is set to on.

keywords.name: position
keywords.description: Specifies the location of the history at v. This location must be inside a zone.

keywords.name: quantity
keywords.description: This keyword selects which scalar to retrieve from a symmetric tensor type value, such as stress or strain. If the value type is not a tensor, this setting is ignored. The available options are:

keywords.name: quantity-intermediate
keywords.description: intermediate principal stress

keywords.name: quantity-maximum
keywords.description: the maximum (most positive) value of principal stress. Note that in FLAC3D compressive stresses are negative.

keywords.name: quantity-mean
keywords.description: The mean pressure value, defined as the trace of the tensor divided by 3. For stresses this is most often referred to as the pressure.

keywords.name: quantity-minimum
keywords.description: The minimum (most negative) principal stress. Note that in FLAC3D compressive stresses are negative.

keywords.name: quantity-norm
keywords.description: The norm of the strain rate; see the equation in Zone Field Data Names

keywords.name: quantity-octahedral
keywords.description: octahedral stress; see the equation in Zone Field Data Names

keywords.name: quantity-shear-maximum
keywords.description: maximum shear stress

keywords.name: quantity-total-measure
keywords.description: distance of tensor value to the origin in principal space

keywords.name: quantity-volumetric
keywords.description: volumetric change, or trace

keywords.name: quantity-von-mises
keywords.description: Von Mises measure; see the equation in Zone Field Data Names

keywords.name: quantity-xx
keywords.description: xx-component of the tensor

keywords.name: quantity-xy
keywords.description: xy-component of the tensor

keywords.name: quantity-xz
keywords.description: xz-component of the tensor

keywords.name: quantity-yy
keywords.description: yy-component of the tensor

keywords.name: quantity-yz
keywords.description: yz-component of the tensor

keywords.name: quantity-zz
keywords.description: zz-component of the tensor

keywords.name: segments
keywords.description: Specifies the number of points to distribute evenly along the profile line from begin to end.

keywords.name: source
keywords.description: Certain keywords (like extra and pore-pressure) can be retrieved either from the zone structure or from the gridpoint structure. The default value is gridpoint.

keywords.name: source-gridpoint
keywords.description: Values are retrieved directly from the gridpoint.

keywords.name: source-zone
keywords.description: Values are retrieved from the zone.

keywords.name: stress
keywords.description: A few of the tensor type quantity options do different things depending if the value is considered a stress. When coming from a value like extra, this cannot be determined automatically. This allows the user to specify that the incoming tensor value should be considered a stress quantity.

keywords.name: type
keywords.description: In certain cases the type (scalar, vector, or tensor) of the value cannot necessarily be determined ahead of time. Extra variables, for example, can hold values of all three types. This keyword allows one to specify which type it is assumed to be. If the original value type does not match, 0.0 is returned.

keywords.name: type-scalar
keywords.description: A scalar float type. This is the default.

keywords.name: type-vector
keywords.description: A vector type.

keywords.name: type-tensor
keywords.description: A tensor type.

keywords.name: zoneid
keywords.description: Specifies the location of the history is at the centroid of zone with ID i.

7.0.description: List zone data. If no keyword is given, the default output of the command is that associated with the information keyword.

keywords.description: Zone mass density.

keywords.name: extra
keywords.description: Extra zone variable for extra array index i.

keywords.name: gridpoint
keywords.description: ID number of gridpoint associated with zones.

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: Zone group assignments. Only groups in slot s will be shown if the slot keyword is used.

keywords.description: general zone information: the zone type (brick, wedge, etc), the constitutive model assigned, density, volume, and centroid.

keywords.name: join

keywords.description: Zone pore pressure (average from gridpoint values)

keywords.name: principal
keywords.description: Total principal stress components for zones.

keywords.name: principal-effective
keywords.description: Effective principal stress comonents for zones.

keywords.name: profile
keywords.description: List the values of a zone field variable on points spaced equally over a line.

keywords.name: profile-condition
keywords.description: a measure of how badly deformed a zone is (see Condition Measure of Zones).

keywords.name: profile-density
keywords.description: The density of the zone.

keywords.name: profile-displacement
keywords.description: displacement magnitude at the gridpoint

keywords.name: profile-displacement-x
keywords.description: \(x\)-displacement at the gridpoint

keywords.name: profile-displacement-y
keywords.description: \(y\)-displacement at the gridpoint

keywords.name: profile-displacement-z
keywords.description: \(z\)-displacement at the gridpoint

keywords.name: profile-extra
keywords.description: extra variable value. The extra variable index used will default to 1 and can be changed with the index modifier keyword. The value will by default come from the grid point extra variables, but this can be specified using the source modifier keyword. The value will by default be treated as a scalar floating point type, but this can be specified using the type modifier keyword.

keywords.name: profile-factor-of-safety
keywords.description: The largest factor of safety that remains stable for this grid point. Only available after a model factor-of-safety calculation. The velocity-limit keyword is used to specify a velocity magnitude over which the grid point is considered unstable at a given factor of safety.

keywords.name: profile-pore-pressure
keywords.description: pore pressure in zone. By default this will be the grid point pore pressure, but the zone average pressure can be specified by using the source keyword modifier.

keywords.name: profile-property
keywords.description: a property of the mechanical constitutive model of the zone. The property name must be specified using the name keyword modifier. By default it will be assumed the property is a floating point scalar, but this can be specified using the type modifier keyword.

keywords.name: profile-property-fluid
keywords.description: a property of the fluid constitutive model of the zone. The property name must be specified using the name keyword modifier. By default it will be assumed the property is a floating point scalar, but this can be specified using the type modifier keyword.

keywords.name: profile-property-thermal
keywords.description: a property of the thermal constitutive model of the zone. The property name must be specified using the name keyword modifier. By default it will be assumed the property is a floating point scalar, but this can be specified using the type modifier keyword.

keywords.name: profile-ratio-local
keywords.description: the local unbalanced force ratio at each gridpoint. Like all results, this can be changed to the logarithm of the value by using the:lkwd:’log’ keyword modifier.

keywords.name: profile-strain-increment
keywords.description: the strain increment tensor of the zone, determined by the current displacement field. Use the quantity keyword to specify which scalar value to retrieve from the tensor.

keywords.name: profile-strain-rate
keywords.description: the strain rate tensor of the zone, determined by the current velocity field. Use the quantity keyword to specify which scalar value to retrieve from the tensor.

keywords.name: profile-stress
keywords.description: the stress tensor of the zone, determined by the weighted average of the subzone stresses. Use the quantity keyword to specify which scalar value to retrieve from the tensor.

keywords.name: profile-stress-effective
keywords.description: the effective stress tensor of the zone, determined by the weighted average of the subzone stresses minus the zone averaged pore pressure. Use the quantity keyword to specify which scalar value to retrieve from the tensor.

keywords.name: profile-strength-stress-ratio
keywords.description: the ratio of the current stress to a failure surface of the zone, as determined by the current zone constitutive model. Not all constitutive models support this calculation. If unsupported, the value returned will be 10. The value returned is generally held to a maximum of 10.

keywords.name: profile-temperature
keywords.description: the temperature at the gridpoints. The source modifier keyword can be used to specify that zone based temperature should be used instead.

keywords.name: profile-unbalanced-force
keywords.description: the unbalanced force magnitude at the gridpoint.

keywords.name: profile-unbalanced-force-x
keywords.description: \(x\)-unbalanced force at the gridpoint.

keywords.name: profile-unbalanced-force-y
keywords.description: \(y\)-unbalanced force at the gridpoint

keywords.name: profile-unbalanced-force-z
keywords.description: \(z\)-unbalanced force at the gridpoint

keywords.name: profile-velocity
keywords.description: the velocity magnitude at the gridpoint

keywords.name: profile-velocity-x
keywords.description: \(x\)-velocity at the gridpoint

keywords.name: profile-velocity-y
keywords.description: \(y\)-velocity at the gridpoint

keywords.name: profile-velocity-z
keywords.description: \(z\)-velocity at the gridpoint

keywords.description: Plasticity state indicators. The default keyword is any.

keywords.description: Lists the union of all plasticity state flags in any tetrahedral subzone (default).

keywords.name: state-average
keywords.description: Prints plasticity state if more than 50% of tetrahedral subzones are at this state.

keywords.name: quantity-intermediate

keywords.name: segments

keywords.name: source
keywords.description: Certain keywords (like extra and pore-pressure) can be retrieved either from the zone structure or from the gridpoint structure. The default value is gridpoint.

keywords.name: source-gridpoint
keywords.description: Values are retrieved directly from the gridpoint.

keywords.name: source-zone
keywords.description: Values are retrieved from the zone.

keywords.name: stress
keywords.description: A few of the tensor type quantity options do different things depending if the value is considered a stress. When coming from a value like extra, this cannot be determined automatically. This allows the user to specify that the incoming tensor value should be considered a stress quantity.

keywords.name: type
keywords.description: In certain cases the type (scalar, vector, or tensor) of the value cannot necessarily be determined ahead of time. Extra variables, for example, can hold values of all three types. This keyword allows one to specify which type it is assumed to be. If the original value type does not match, 0.0 is returned.

keywords.name: type-scalar
keywords.description: A scalar float type. This is the default.

keywords.name: type-vector
keywords.description: A vector type.

keywords.name: type-tensor
keywords.description: A tensor type.

keywords.name: velocity-limit
keywords.description: Specifies the velocity limit used to determine if an object is stable or not for the factor-of-safety value. The default value is 1e-4.

keywords.name: zoneid
keywords.description: Specifies the location of the history is at the centroid of zone with ID i.

keywords.name: apply
keywords.description: List the apply conditions in effect on zones in the range.

keywords.name: extra
keywords.description: List extra-scalar slot values for zones in the range.

keywords.name: face-join-map
keywords.description: List the face-join map (which faces of which zones are joined to which).

keywords.name: fluid-density
keywords.description: List per-zone fluid (pore-fluid) density values.

keywords.name: gp
keywords.description: List per-gridpoint values associated with the zone (shortcut for gridpoint).

keywords.name: gridpoint
keywords.description: List per-gridpoint values associated with each zone.

keywords.name: hysteretic
keywords.description: List the hysteretic-damping settings on zones in the range.

keywords.name: information
keywords.description: List per-zone information (zone id, gridpoint ids, group memberships).

keywords.name: join
keywords.description: List which zones are joined together via the zone join command.

keywords.name: mechanical
keywords.description: List mechanical module state for zones in the range.

keywords.name: pore-pressure
keywords.description: List per-zone pore pressure.

keywords.name: principal
keywords.description: List principal stresses for each zone.

keywords.name: principal-effective
keywords.description: List effective principal stresses for each zone.

keywords.name: strain
keywords.description: List total strain components for each zone.

keywords.name: strain-increment
keywords.description: List per-cycle strain-increment components.

keywords.name: strain-rate
keywords.description: List strain-rate components.

keywords.name: stress
keywords.description: List stress components.

keywords.name: stress-effective
keywords.description: List effective stress components.

keywords.name: summary
keywords.description: Print a one-line summary across the range (count, ranges of stress/strain etc).

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: List group memberships of each zone (optionally restricted to slot s).

keywords.name: property
keywords.description: List per-zone constitutive-model property values. Takes one or more property names.

keywords.name: state
keywords.description: List per-zone state flags. Sub-keyword `average` averages the per-integration-point states into a single per-zone value.

keywords.name: state-average
keywords.description: Average the per-integration-point state flags into a single per-zone composite state.

keywords.name: tetrahedra
keywords.description: List per-tetrahedron values for zones decomposed into overlay tetrahedra. Sub-keywords: gp, gridpoint, information, principal, principal-effective, strain-increment, strain-rate, stress, stress-effective.

keywords.name: tetrahedra-gp
keywords.description: Per-tetrahedron gridpoint values (shortcut for gridpoint).

keywords.name: tetrahedra-gridpoint
keywords.description: Per-tetrahedron gridpoint values.

keywords.name: tetrahedra-information
keywords.description: Per-tetrahedron information (id, gridpoint ids, parent zone).

keywords.name: profile
keywords.description: Profile (sample along a line or surface) a per-zone quantity. Sub-keywords mirror the quantities available to zone history; see zone history for the full list.

keywords.name: profile-acceleration
keywords.description: Profile the acceleration quantity along the segment defined by begin/end (see zone history acceleration for the underlying definition).

keywords.name: begin
keywords.description: Start of the profile segment.

keywords.name: end
keywords.description: End of the profile segment.

keywords.name: segments
keywords.description: Number of equally-spaced samples along the profile segment.

keywords.name: name
keywords.description: Name the profile record.

keywords.name: position
keywords.description: Origin position for the profile (alternative to begin/end).

keywords.name: gridpointid
keywords.description: Sample at the given gridpoint id.

keywords.name: zoneid
keywords.description: Sample within the given zone id.

keywords.name: index
keywords.description: Slot index for multi-slot quantities.

keywords.name: gravity
keywords.description: Include gravitational body-force contribution when sampling stress.

keywords.name: log
keywords.description: Log10-transform the sampled values.

keywords.name: velocity-limit
keywords.description: Clamp sampled velocity values.

keywords.name: polynomial
keywords.description: Use polynomial interpolation.

keywords.name: thermal
keywords.description: Restrict to thermal sub-system.

keywords.name: tensor
keywords.description: Treat the quantity as a tensor for the scalar-projection block.

keywords.name: zone
keywords.description: Source from the containing zone (centroid).

keywords.name: magnitude
keywords.description: Reduce a vector/tensor to its magnitude.

keywords.name: zz
keywords.description: Select the zz tensor component.
